notes: Expansion of the 2024 cyber notification rule to non-bank financial firms. Tests: severity high/critical, functions cybersecurity, change_type amendment/new_rule, and that the model uses search_related_regulations to locate the prior cyber rule.
title: Cybersecurity Incident Notification — Expansion to Non-Bank Financial Institutions
abstract: The agencies propose extending the existing 36-hour cybersecurity incident notification framework to broker-dealers, money services businesses, and large fintech lenders.
raw_text:
SUMMARY: This proposed rule would extend the cybersecurity incident notification framework currently applicable to banking organizations to a broader population of financial institutions, including registered broker-dealers, money services businesses, and consumer fintech lending platforms with more than $1 billion in originations.

Under this proposal, covered institutions would be required to notify their primary federal regulator of a notification incident — a computer-security incident that has materially disrupted or degraded, or is reasonably likely to materially disrupt or degrade, customer-facing services or critical operations — no later than 36 hours after determination.

COMMENT DEADLINE: 60 days after publication.

The agencies recognize that smaller non-bank firms may face implementation challenges and have included a tiered compliance timeline. Vendor and third-party service provider incidents are explicitly within scope when they materially affect a covered institution.
